t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Real-account Customer ID. HASH distribution key. References `Dim_Customer.RealCID`. Each customer has one real CID.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Real-account Customer ID. HASH distribution key in source. References `Dim_Customer.RealCID`. Each customer has one real CID.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Added 'in source' qualifier — contextually accurate since BI_DB table is ROUND_ROBIN not HASH

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Withdrawal request ID for cashout events. 0 for non-cashout events.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Withdrawal request ID for cashout events. 0 for non-cashout events. Part of the clustered index.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Added local index context not present in upstream

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: UTC timestamp when the action occurred. For position opens: when position was opened. For logins: login time. For credits: when the credit was recorded.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: UTC timestamp when the action occurred. For credits: when the credit was recorded.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Trimmed position-open and login context (inapplicable to cashout-only table)

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Short code for the regulation. Used in V_Dim_Customer and analytics dashboards. Values match production Dictionary.Regulation.Name.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Short code for the regulation. Used in V_Dim_Customer and analytics dashboards. Values match production Dictionary.Regulation.Name. Passthrough from Dim_Regulation. 11 distinct values in 2025: CySEC, FCA, FSA Seychelles, FinCEN+FINRA, ASIC & GAML, FSRA, BVI, ASIC, eToroUS, MAS, FinCEN.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Tier display name: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A. Used in BackOffice reporting JOINs and customer-facing UI.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Tier display name: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A. Used in BackOffice reporting JOINs and customer-facing UI. Passthrough from Dim_PlayerLevel.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Full country name in English. Unique per row. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Full country name in English. Unique per row in Dim_Country. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports. Passthrough from Dim_Country. 147 distinct values in 2025 data.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Changed 'Unique per row' to 'Unique per row in Dim_Country'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Human-readable account type label. Maps to Dictionary.AccountType.AccountTypeName in production (renamed in DWH). Used in reporting to display account classification.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Human-readable account type label. Maps to Dictionary.AccountType.AccountTypeName in production (renamed in DWH). Used in reporting to display account classification. Passthrough from Dim_AccountType.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Human-readable PI tier name. Values: No, Certified, Cadet, Rising Star, Champion, Elite, Elite Pro, Removed, Rejected. Used in BackOffice customer views, Trade procedures, and SalesForce integration.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Human-readable PI tier name. Values: No, Certified, Cadet, Rising Star, Champion, Elite, Elite Pro, Removed, Rejected. Used in BackOffice customer views, Trade procedures, and SalesForce integration. Passthrough from Dim_GuruStatus.

issues.problem: Upstream Fact_CustomerAction description trimmed: dropped 'For position opens: when position was opened. For logins: login time.' — defensible for a cashout-only table but deviates from strict verbatim Tier 1 inheritance.
No explicit Phase Gate Checklist in wiki body. Footer says 'Phases: 11/14' but does not detail which phases were completed or skipped.
Upstream Fact_CustomerAction declares WithdrawID as int (NO NULL), but DDL and wiki show bigint (NULL). Type widening is correctly reflected but not called out.
Gotcha says 'predominantly 0.0' but provides no percentage or count of non-zero rows — an auditor would benefit from specifics.
Referenced By only lists SP_EY_Audit_CashoutFees. Any downstream consumers (reports, views) are not mentioned.
